canonicalSourcesChecked: recent coffee-app, coffee-scraper, and purveyors-cli PRs since 2026-04-19

activeAlignmentRisks.risk: api-architecture still has no published post on origin/main despite being central to the product vision
evidence: Published pillar balance remains agentic-stack 7, supply-chain 3, coffee-data-pipeline 2, ai-first-product 1, market-intelligence 1, api-architecture 0.
Recent work keeps moving toward API-first platform contracts: /v1/catalog hardening, ADR-005 access levels, /v1/price-index, structured process filters, and CLI parity.
activeAlignmentRisks.recommendedAction: After the active moats PR is resolved, draft an api-architecture post before another agentic-stack or broad AI essay.

activeAlignmentRisks.risk: open blog PR queue is carrying too many parallel drafts and stale Coffee Intelligence pieces
evidence: Open blog PRs include #301, #287, #285, #267, #228, plus outline-only PR #323.
#285, #267, and #228 are older Wednesday Coffee Intelligence drafts whose data snapshots predate recent price-index, process-transparency, and proof-layer work.
activeAlignmentRisks.recommendedAction: Prefer finishing or closing/rebasing existing draft PRs before creating another market-intelligence draft. Treat stale snapshot data as a credibility risk.

activeAlignmentRisks.risk: the active backlog is healthy but source-map bookkeeping lagged behind the last week of platform work
evidence: Since then, price-index API, proof-layer planning, certification taxonomy planning, access-tier positioning, process transparency docs, and CLI structured process filters landed.
activeAlignmentRisks.recommendedAction: Capture the new ideas and next-post rationale in the source map instead of relying on git history as the memory layer.

newIdeas.title: Proof passports for coffee claims: why the next marketplace unit is evidence, not listings
newIdeas.whyNow: The product now has structured process fields, producer evidence extraction, catalog access tiers, and proof-summary implementation plans. The counterintuitive angle is that trustworthy claim evidence may become the monetizable layer above listing visibility.

newIdeas.title: Certification data needs a schema, not a substring search
newIdeas.source: coffee-scraper notes/implementation-plans/2026-04-29-certifications-taxonomy-schema.md; coffee-scraper PR #234; coffee-app DEVLOG certification taxonomy item
newIdeas.whyNow: Five straight audit emails and a production sample found broad certification evidence trapped in overloaded fields. This is a clean example of schema design turning messy supplier copy into buyer and agent leverage.

newIdeas.title: We Built a Real-Time Specialty Coffee Price Index
newIdeas.whyNow: The /v1/price-index endpoint has shipped. The launch post should wait until CLI/docs conversion work makes the cross-surface story complete, but the idea is no longer just a future implementation plan.

publicationCadence.status: cadence risk by visible publication dates, mitigated by several open complete draft PRs
publicationCadence.notes: The most useful next action is not more ideation. It is resolving the open publish-ready queue, then drafting the missing api-architecture piece.

backlogHealth.status: strong volume, weak pillar balance, increasing platform/API evidence quality
notes: API architecture is the clearest gap against PRODUCT_VISION.
Market-intelligence now has stronger raw material from /v1/price-index but should not ship from stale draft snapshots.
Coffee-data-pipeline has fresh schema-governance material from certifications and proof/evidence work.

nextPriorityPosts.title: Which Moats Survive When AI Makes Software Cheap?
nextPriorityPosts.whyNow: It is already drafted with hero, source links, and a 1,199-word self-edit. Ship or close it before opening more full drafts.

nextPriorityPosts.title: What If the Real Marketplace Is the Listing Schema?
nextPriorityPosts.whyNow: It closes the zero-post api-architecture gap and now has fresh proof from /v1/catalog, ADR-004, ADR-005, structured process fields, and the public/API/CLI access split.

nextPriorityPosts.title: We Built a Real-Time Specialty Coffee Price Index
nextPriorityPosts.whyNow: The price-index API has shipped and would make the market-intelligence pillar concrete. Wait for CLI/docs bridge or explicitly frame the post as the API contract launch, not a fully complete product launch.

publishedPostMaintenanceDecision.reason: The run found no new broken links or materially false strategic claims beyond the existing 2026-04-26 drift ledger. Current guidance is to avoid churning old posts unless there is a real credibility issue.
